channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump comments on Iran ceasefire request; commercial LPG and jet fuel prices see rise in India
item.description: In recent developments, former US President Donald Trump claimed that Iran's 'new regime president' has requested a ceasefire. Trump did not specify clearly, as the current Iranian president, Masoud P...

item.title: NEST boosts transparency and efficiency in Tanzania's public procurement
item.description: Tanzania's government dey see big improvements since dem start using the National e-Procurement System (NEST). For the past two years, government institutions don save over Sh35 billion, combining bot...

item.title: Michael Patrick King discusses the return of The Comeback and reflections on And Just Like That
item.description: Michael Patrick King, renowned for his work on Sex and the City and its spin-off And Just Like That, has announced the return of The Comeback for a third and final season, co-created with Lisa Kudrow....

item.title: Police search for gunman after serious shooting in Bristol
item.description: A serious incident occurred in Bristol when a man in his twenties sustained potentially life-changing injuries after being shot in his car on Speedwell Road at around 13:47 BST. Avon and Somerset Poli...

item.title: Amaroo School temporarily closes classrooms due to mould
item.description: Mould was detected in the Unakita Building at Amaroo School in Canberra, prompting the temporary closure of some classrooms while remediation work is undertaken after hours. The ACT Education Director...

item.title: The Urgent Need to Address Trump’s Global Economic Impact
item.description: During his second term, Donald Trump's actions have increasingly destabilised global politics and economics. Policies such as imposing and retracting tariffs reflected inconsistent economic strategies...

item.title: Supreme Court questions Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for US-born children
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has expressed skepticism regarding former President Donald Trump's legal attempt to end birthright citizenship, a policy that grants citizenship to anyone born on Ameri...

item.title: Four Arrested in Connection with KuGompo City Woman’s Murder
item.description: Four suspects have been apprehended in connection with the tragic death of 30-year-old Zovuyo Mbana from KuGompo City. Mbana went missing last week, prompting family and friends to launch an urgent se...

item.title: UK minimum wage increases to £12.71, prompting mixed reactions from workers and businesses
item.description: The UK national minimum wage has risen by 50p to £12.71 per hour for those over 21, affecting around 2.7 million workers. Young adults aged 18-20 will see an 85p increase to £10.85, while under-18s an...

item.title: UDA Delays Phase II Grassroots Elections to Facilitate Member Registration
item.description: The United Democratic Alliance (UDA) has officially pushed back its Repeat Phase II grassroots elections by a fortnight to accommodate party members who requested more time to register and confirm the...

item.title: Latra Reminds Bus Operators to Follow Rules Before Raising Fares Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: The Land Transport Regulatory Authority (Latra) imeonya operators wa mabasi dhidi ya ku-increase fares bila approval wakati fuel prices yamepandishwa sana nchini Tanzania. Latra Director General, Habi...

item.title: Chelsea posts record pre-tax loss of £262.4m despite strong revenue
item.description: Chelsea Football Club has reported a pre-tax loss of £262.4 million for the 2024-25 season, marking the largest loss in Premier League history. The significant deficit contrasts with the previous year...

item.title: Tanzanian CEOs Confident About Growth but Urge Faster Digital Transformation
item.description: Tanzania's top business leaders dey optimistic about economic growth, but dem still cautious about challenges ahead. PwC Tanzania's latest CEO Survey show say 65% of CEOs dey expect good revenue growt...

item.title: Tiger Woods Granted Permission to Travel Abroad for Medical Treatment
item.description: Tiger Woods, the famous golfer, don get permission from one Florida court judge to waka go abroad for proper medical treatment after him dey arrested for suspicion of driving under the influence (DUI)...

item.title: The Forgotten Timepiece of President Ramon Magsaysay: A Historical Exploration
item.description: This article explores the story behind President Ramon Magsaysay’s long-forgotten watch, a piece that once symbolized his leadership era in the Philippines. Known for his hands-on approach and deep co...

item.title: How Africa Can Navigate the Economic Impact of the Iran War
item.description: The ongoing conflict initiated by Israel and the United States against Iran has caused widespread global instability, with African nations particularly vulnerable to its economic side effects. The war...

item.title: Tourist arrivals in Baguio drop by half due to rising fuel costs
item.description: Baguio City has reported a significant 50 percent drop in tourist arrivals, which local officials have attributed to rising fuel prices. Mayor Benjamin Magalong explained that the higher transportatio...

item.title: NASA prepares for historic Artemis II mission, sending astronauts on a 10-day lunar journey
item.description: NASA has initiated the final countdown for its Artemis II mission, marking the first human journey towards the moon in over fifty years. The launch, scheduled from Kennedy Space Center in Florida, inv...

item.title: Carlton’s struggles under Voss highlight coaching and tactical challenges
item.description: Michael Voss, Carlton’s head coach, has been given extensive support and resources to succeed, but the Blues continue to suffer recurring collapses in matches. Despite early wins, Carlton has repeated...

item.title: Real Sociedad Provides Injury Update on Job Ochieng After Harambee Stars Duty
item.description: Kenya's Harambee Stars striker Job Ochieng has been reported to have a muscular injury at the back of his left thigh, which he picked while playing against Estonia in the FIFA Series held in Rwanda. T...
